1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# API smoke tests: verify all registered routes respond with correct HTTP status codes
3	# Requires the full stack running via docker-compose
4	# Note: Most endpoints now require authentication, so they return 401 instead of 501
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	BASE_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
9	PASSED=0
10	FAILED=0
11	
12	check_endpoint() {
13	    local method="$1"
14	    local path="$2"
15	    local expected_status="$3"
16	    local url="$BASE_URL$path"
17	
18	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X "$method" "$url")
19	
20	    if [ "$STATUS" = "$expected_status" ]; then
21	        echo "  PASS  $method $path -> $STATUS"
22	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
23	    else
24	        echo "  FAIL  $method $path -> $STATUS (expected $expected_status)"
25	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
26	    fi
27	}
28	
29	echo "API Smoke Tests"
30	echo "==============="
31	echo "Target: $BASE_URL"
32	echo ""
33	
34	# Health endpoints (public, no auth)
35	check_endpoint GET "/api/health/live" "200"
36	check_endpoint GET "/api/health/ready" "200"
37	
38	# Auth endpoints
39	check_endpoint GET "/api/auth/me" "401"
40	
41	# Protected endpoints (expect 401 without token)
42	# Each path targets a concrete mounted handler so the auth guard fires.
43	check_endpoint GET  "/api/admin/org/" "401"
44	check_endpoint GET  "/api/users/" "401"
45	check_endpoint GET  "/api/roles/" "401"
46	check_endpoint GET  "/api/catalog/" "401"
47	check_endpoint GET  "/api/packages/" "401"
48	check_endpoint GET  "/api/plans/" "401"
49	check_endpoint GET  "/api/delivery/" "401"
50	check_endpoint GET  "/api/billing/invoices" "401"
51	check_endpoint GET  "/api/payments/" "401"
52	check_endpoint GET  "/api/scoring/templates" "401"
53	check_endpoint POST "/api/reports/export" "401"
54	check_endpoint GET  "/api/audit/" "401"
55	check_endpoint GET  "/api/ops/flags" "401"
56	
57	echo ""
58	echo "Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"
59	
60	if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
61	    echo "(Failures may indicate backend is not running)"
62	    exit 1
63	fi
64

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# API tests: Authentication, authorization boundaries, and security endpoints
3	# Requires the full stack running via docker-compose
4	
5	set -euo pipefail
6	
7	BASE_URL="${BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8000}"
8	PASSED=0
9	FAILED=0
10	
11	check() {
12	    local name="$1"
13	    local expected="$2"
14	    local actual="$3"
15	
16	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
17	        echo "  PASS  $name (got $actual)"
18	        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
19	    else
20	        echo "  FAIL  $name (expected $expected, got $actual)"
21	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
22	    fi
23	}
24	
25	echo "==========================="
26	echo " Auth & Security API Tests"
27	echo "==========================="
28	echo "Target: $BASE_URL"
29	echo ""
30	
31	# --- Test 1: Login with valid credentials ---
32	echo "[1] Login with valid credentials"
33	LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
34	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
35	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"Admin123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
36	
37	if echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -q '"token"'; then
38	    TOKEN=$(echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
39	    check "Login returns token" "true" "true"
40	else
41	    check "Login returns token" "true" "false"
42	    TOKEN=""
43	fi
44	
45	# --- Test 2: Login with invalid password ---
46	echo "[2] Login with invalid password"
47	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
48	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
49	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrong"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
50	check "Invalid password returns 401" "401" "$STATUS"
51	
52	# --- Test 3: Login with nonexistent user ---
53	echo "[3] Login with nonexistent user"
54	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
55	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
56	    -d '{"username":"nouser","password":"test"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
57	check "Nonexistent user returns 401" "401" "$STATUS"
58	
59	# --- Test 4: Access protected endpoint without token ---
60	echo "[4] Access /api/users without token"
61	STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$BASE_URL/api/users/")
62	check "No token returns 401" "401" "$STATUS"
63	
64	# --- Test 5: Access protected endpoint with valid token ---
65	echo "[5] Access /api/users with admin token"
66	if [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
67	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
68	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
69	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
70	    check "Admin can access users" "200" "$STATUS"
71	else
72	    echo "  SKIP (no token from login)"
73	fi
74	
75	# --- Test 6: Get current user profile ---
76	echo "[6] GET /api/auth/me with admin token"
77	if [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
78	    ME_RESP=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/auth/me" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
79	    if echo "$ME_RESP" | grep -q '"permissions"'; then
80	        check "Current user has permissions array" "true" "true"
81	    else
82	        check "Current user has permissions array" "true" "false"
83	    fi
84	else
85	    echo "  SKIP (no token)"
86	fi
87	
88	# --- Test 7: Unauthorized role cannot access admin endpoints ---
89	echo "[7] Auditor cannot write users"
90	AUDITOR_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
91	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
92	    -d '{"username":"auditor","password":"Auditor123!"}' 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
93	AUDITOR_TOKEN=$(echo "$AUDITOR_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
94

99	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/" \
100	        -d '{"username":"test","email":"test@test.com","password":"Test123!","org_id":"fake"}' \
101	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
102	    check "Auditor cannot create users (403)" "403" "$STATUS"
103	else
104	    echo "  SKIP (auditor login failed)"
105	fi

183	fi

186	echo "[13] GET roles for nonexistent user returns 404"
187	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
188	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
189	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
190	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/roles" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
191	    check "Nonexistent user roles returns 404" "404" "$STATUS"
192	else

197	echo "[14] GET scopes for nonexistent user returns 404"
198	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
199	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
200	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \

202	    check "Nonexistent user scopes returns 404" "404" "$STATUS"

222	echo "[16] Coach cannot revoke roles from users"
223	if [ -n "$COACH_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ADMIN_ID" ]; then
224	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
225	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \

229	else

231	fi
232	
233	# --- Test 17: Coach cannot manage scopes (lacks permission) ---
234	echo "[17] Coach cannot read user scopes"
235	if [ -n "$COACH_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ADMIN_ID" ]; then
236	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
237	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \
238	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ADMIN_ID/scopes" \
239	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
240	    check "Coach cannot read user scopes (403)" "403" "$STATUS"
241	else
242	    echo "  SKIP (coach login or admin id unavailable)"
243	fi
244	
245	# --- Test 18: Coach cannot assign scopes (lacks permission) ---
246	echo "[18] Coach cannot assign scopes to users"
247	if [ -n "$COACH_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ADMIN_ID" ]; then

249	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $COACH_TOKEN" \

251	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ADMIN_ID/scopes" \
252	        -d '{"org_id":"fake-org","access_level":"read"}' \
253	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
254	    check "Coach cannot assign scopes (403)" "403" "$STATUS"
255	else

259	# --- Test 19: Auditor cannot assign roles (lacks permission — read-only role) ---
260	echo "[19] Auditor cannot assign roles"
261	if [ -n "$AUDITOR_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ADMIN_ID" ]; then

263	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUDITOR_TOKEN" \
264	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
265	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ADMIN_ID/roles" \

267	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
268	    check "Auditor cannot assign roles (403)" "403" "$STATUS"
269	else

271	fi

274	echo "[20] DELETE scope for nonexistent user returns 404"
275	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then

277	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \

279	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")

281	else

283	fi

286	echo "[21] POST role for nonexistent user returns 404"
287	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
288	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
289	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \

299	# =============================================================================
300	# Deterministic cross-org tenant isolation (tests 22-26)
301	#
302	# Strategy:
303	#   1. Admin (System Administrator — bypasses all scopes) creates org-B.
304	#   2. Admin creates user-B inside org-B.
305	#   3. Admin discovers org-A (the seeded demo org) and the Operations Manager
306	#      role (which carries api.users.read but NOT System Administrator bypass).
307	#   4. Admin creates test-actor inside org-A, assigns Ops Manager role + scope
308	#      limited to org-A.
309	#   5. Test-actor tries to read user-B (in org-B) → 403 from data-scope check.
310	#   6. Test-actor reads own-org user (admin, in org-A) → 200 (positive control).
311	#
312	# This proves the data-scope enforcement layer blocks cross-org access for
313	# a privileged-but-scoped actor, independent of the permission layer.
314	# =============================================================================
315	
316	echo ""
317	echo "--- Deterministic Cross-Org Isolation (tests 22-26) ---"
318	
319	# Discover org-A (seeded demo org)
320	ORG_A_ID=""
321	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
322	    ORG_A_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
323	        "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/" 2>/dev/null \
324	        | python3 -c "import sys,json; orgs=json.load(sys.stdin); print(orgs[0]['id'] if orgs else '')" \
325	        2>/dev/null || echo "")
326	fi
327	
328	# Create org-B
329	ORG_B_ID=""
330	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
331	    ORG_B_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/admin/org/" \
332	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
333	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
334	        -d "{\"name\":\"CrossOrgTest Org B $(date +%s)\"}" \
335	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
336	    ORG_B_ID=$(echo "$ORG_B_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
337	fi
338	
339	# Create user-B in org-B
340	USER_B_ID=""
341	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ORG_B_ID" ]; then
342	    USER_B_NAME="crossorg_userb_$(date +%s)"
343	    USER_B_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/" \
344	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
345	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
346	        -d "{\"username\":\"$USER_B_NAME\",\"email\":\"${USER_B_NAME}@test.local\",\"password\":\"UserB123!\",\"org_id\":\"$ORG_B_ID\"}" \
347	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
348	    USER_B_ID=$(echo "$USER_B_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
349	fi
350	
351	# Find the Operations Manager role ID (has api.users.read, NOT System Admin)
352	OPS_MGR_ROLE_ID=""
353	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
354	    OPS_MGR_ROLE_ID=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" "$BASE_URL/api/roles/" 2>/dev/null \
355	        | python3 -c "
356	import sys,json
357	roles=json.load(sys.stdin)
358	for r in roles:
359	    if r['name']=='Operations Manager':
360	        print(r['id']); break
361	" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
362	fi
363	
364	# Create test-actor in org-A
365	ACTOR_TOKEN=""
366	ACTOR_ID=""
367	if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$ORG_A_ID" ] && [ -n "$OPS_MGR_ROLE_ID" ]; then
368	    ACTOR_NAME="crossorg_actor_$(date +%s)"
369	    ACTOR_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/" \
370	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
371	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
372	        -d "{\"username\":\"$ACTOR_NAME\",\"email\":\"${ACTOR_NAME}@test.local\",\"password\":\"Actor123!\",\"org_id\":\"$ORG_A_ID\"}" \
373	        2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
374	    ACTOR_ID=$(echo "$ACTOR_RESP" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
375	
376	    if [ -n "$ACTOR_ID" ]; then
377	        # Assign Operations Manager role
378	        curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ACTOR_ID/roles" \
379	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
380	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
381	            -d "{\"role_id\":\"$OPS_MGR_ROLE_ID\"}" 2>/dev/null > /dev/null
382	
383	        # Grant data scope for org-A ONLY (no scope for org-B)
384	        curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ACTOR_ID/scopes" \
385	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
386	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
387	            -d "{\"org_id\":\"$ORG_A_ID\",\"access_level\":\"write\"}" \
388	            2>/dev/null > /dev/null
389	
390	        # Login as the scoped test-actor
391	        ACTOR_LOGIN=$(curl -sf -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
392	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
393	            -d "{\"username\":\"$ACTOR_NAME\",\"password\":\"Actor123!\"}" \
394	            2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL")
395	        ACTOR_TOKEN=$(echo "$ACTOR_LOGIN" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
396	    fi
397	fi
398	
399	# Run the cross-org assertions
400	if [ -n "$ACTOR_TOKEN" ] && [ -n "$USER_B_ID" ] && [ -n "$ADMIN_ID" ]; then
401	    echo "[22] Scoped actor CANNOT read user in org-B (cross-org -> 403)"
402	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
403	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTOR_TOKEN" \
404	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/$USER_B_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
405	    check "Cross-org GET user -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
406	
407	    echo "[23] Scoped actor CAN read user in own org-A (positive control -> 200)"
408	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
409	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTOR_TOKEN" \
410	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/$ADMIN_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
411	    check "Same-org GET user -> 200" "200" "$STATUS"
412	
413	    echo "[24] Scoped actor CANNOT list roles for user in org-B (cross-org -> 403)"
414	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
415	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTOR_TOKEN" \
416	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/$USER_B_ID/roles" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
417	    # Ops Manager lacks api.roles.read, so this is 403 from permission check.
418	    # Either way the actor is blocked — scope or permission, the boundary holds.
419	    check "Cross-org GET user roles -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
420	
421	    echo "[25] Scoped actor CANNOT read scopes for user in org-B"
422	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
423	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTOR_TOKEN" \
424	        "$BASE_URL/api/users/$USER_B_ID/scopes" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
425	    check "Cross-org GET user scopes -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
426	
427	    echo "[26] Scoped actor CANNOT assign scope to user in org-B"
428	    STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
429	        -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACTOR_TOKEN" \
430	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
431	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$USER_B_ID/scopes" \
432	        -d "{\"org_id\":\"$ORG_B_ID\",\"access_level\":\"read\"}" \
433	        2>/dev/null || echo "000")
434	    check "Cross-org POST scope -> 403" "403" "$STATUS"
435	else
436	    # Fixture setup failed — report the reason, do not silently skip
437	    echo "[22-26] Cross-org fixture setup:"
438	    [ -z "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ] && echo "  FAIL  admin login failed" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
439	    [ -z "$ORG_A_ID" ]    && echo "  FAIL  could not discover org-A" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
440	    [ -z "$ORG_B_ID" ]    && echo "  FAIL  could not create org-B" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
441	    [ -z "$USER_B_ID" ]   && echo "  FAIL  could not create user-B in org-B" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
442	    [ -z "$OPS_MGR_ROLE_ID" ] && echo "  FAIL  could not find Operations Manager role" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
443	    [ -z "$ACTOR_ID" ]    && echo "  FAIL  could not create test-actor in org-A" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
444	    [ -z "$ACTOR_TOKEN" ] && echo "  FAIL  could not login as test-actor" && FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
445	    # If we get here without ADMIN_TOKEN, earlier tests already failed.
446	    # Bound the skip: these tests REQUIRE a running backend with seed data.
447	    if [ -n "$ADMIN_TOKEN" ]; then
448	        echo "  FAIL  cross-org fixture creation failed — check admin API access"
449	        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
450	    fi
451	fi
452	
453	echo ""
454	echo "==========================="
455	echo "Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed"
456	echo "==========================="
457	
458	if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
459	    echo "(Failures may indicate backend is not running or seed data is missing)"
460	    exit 1
461	fi
462

1	# CareOps Service Billing & Quality Management Portal
2	
3	A locally-deployed portal for managing care service delivery, billing, and quality scoring. Built with Dioxus (Rust/WASM frontend), Rocket (Rust backend), and MySQL.
4	
5	## Tech Stack
6	
7	| Component | Technology | Version |
8	|-----------|-----------|---------|
9	| Frontend | Dioxus (Web/WASM) | 0.6 |
10	| Backend | Rocket | 0.5 |
11	| Database | MySQL | 8.0 |
12	| ORM/Migrations | sqlx | 0.7 |
13	| Auth | Argon2id + JWT | -- |
14	| Encryption | AES-256-GCM | -- |
15	| Container | Docker Compose | -- |
16	
17	## Quick Start
18	
19	```bash
20	docker compose up
21	```
22	
23	That's it. This single command:
24	1. Starts MySQL and waits for it to be healthy
25	2. Builds and starts the Rocket backend (runs migrations and seeds automatically)
26	3. Builds and serves the Dioxus frontend via nginx

43	|----------|----------|------|-------------|

50	
51	### Auth Flow
52	
53	1. `POST /api/auth/login` with `{"username": "...", "password": "..."}` returns JWT token + user profile
54	2. Include token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all subsequent requests
55	3. `POST /api/auth/logout` to invalidate session
56	4. `GET /api/auth/me` to fetch current user profile with permissions
57	
58	### Security Features
59	
60	- Password hashing: Argon2id with random salts
61	- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts -> 15-minute lock
62	- Session revocation: logout immediately invalidates the JWT
63	- Permission cache: changes take effect within 30 seconds
64	- Encryption at rest: AES-256-GCM for sensitive fields
65	- Audit logging: all auth events and security-sensitive mutations
66	- CORS origin allowlist: explicit origins only (no wildcard with credentials). Configure via `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` env var (comma-separated). Defaults to localhost ports in local mode.
67	- Payment idempotency: 5-minute duplicate rejection window per `(org_id, idempotency_key)`. Same key within 5 minutes returns 409 Conflict; after 5 minutes the key is accepted for a new payment. Race-safe via `INSERT … ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` on the `payment_idempotency_keys` table (see migration `20240108000000_restore_idempotency_window.sql` and [Billing & Financial Controls, Section 5](docs/billing_and_financial_controls.md#5-idempotency-model) for full details).
68	
69	See [docs/security_model.md](docs/security_model.md) for the full security architecture.
70	
71	## RBAC Summary
72	
73	Six permission categories enforce access control at every layer:
74	
75	| Category | Enforcement | Example |
76	|----------|-------------|---------|
77	| `menu.*` | Frontend sidebar visibility | `menu.dashboard`, `menu.admin` |
78	| `action.*` | Button/action visibility + backend check | `action.users.create` |
79	| `api.*` | Backend route authorization | `api.users.read`, `api.billing.write` |
80	| Data scope | Backend org/dept/project filtering | `user_data_scopes` table |
81	
82	Frontend permission checks are advisory. Backend enforcement is mandatory.

84	## Observability & Resilience
85	
86	### Startup
87	
88	```bash
89	docker compose up                          # default: chaos drills off
90	CHAOS_ENABLED=true docker compose up       # arm chaos drills (Sunday 02:00 UTC only)
91	```
92	
93	### Degradation toggles
94	
95	Two in-process toggles persist in the `ops_config` DB table and can be flipped without restart:
96	
97	| Toggle key | Default | Effect when disabled |
98	|---|---|---|
99	| `exports_enabled` | `true` | Export endpoint returns 503 |
100	| `analytics_enabled` | `true` | All report endpoints return 503 |
101	
102	**Safety:** Toggle parsing is fail-closed. If a toggle value in the database is missing or malformed (not `"true"`/`"false"`), it defaults to `false` (feature disabled) and emits a warning log.
103	
104	Toggle via `POST /api/ops/flags/:key/enable|disable` (requires `api.ops.write`, System Administrator only).
105	All changes are written to `ops_events` and `audit_log`.
106	
107	### Error-rate alarm
108	
109	A background task evaluates the 10-minute sliding window error rate every 30 seconds.
110	When the rate exceeds **2%**, the alarm transitions to ALERTING and an `ops_events` row is written.
111	Visible at `GET /api/health/alerts` and `GET /api/health/metrics`.
112	
113	### Chaos drills
114	
115	Weekly, Sunday 02:00–02:15 UTC. Guarded by `CHAOS_ENABLED=true` env var (off by default).
116	Simulates 200 ms DB latency and 5% request timeouts — bounded, time-limited faults.
117	See [docs/observability_and_resilience.md](docs/observability_and_resilience.md) for full details.
118	
119	## Migrations & Seeds
120	
121	- **Migrations** run automatically when the backend starts (embedded via sqlx)
122	- **Seeds** are idempotent: checked via `_seed_history` table
123	- Seeds include: permissions, roles with permission mappings, demo org, demo users
124	- No manual database setup is required

1. Change docker-compose.yml db host port to avoid common MySQL conflict (3307:3306)
2. Add a robust wait-for-backend readiness loop at the start of run_tests.sh (backend migrations/seeds take 10-30s after container start)

# --- Wait for backend readiness ---
# When run right after `docker compose up -d`, the backend container may still

        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
    WAITED=$((WAITED + 2))
done
echo """}

## Summary

The test failures had two root causes; both are now fixed.

**1. `docker-compose.yml:12` — host port conflict on MySQL**

The previous commit (`765e301`) set the db mapping to `3306:3306`, but port 3306 is commonly bound by a host-installed MySQL (it was on this machine). When `docker compose up` couldn't bind the port, the db container never started, the backend waited forever on its `service_healthy` dep, and every API test got connection-refused (`status "000"`).

Fixed by mapping host `3307 → container 3306`. The backend still connects internally via `db:3306` on the compose network, so nothing inside the stack changes; only host-side access moves to 3307.

**2. `run_tests.sh` — startup race against the backend**

`run_tests.sh` immediately ran the HTTP tests as soon as it was invoked. After `docker compose up -d`, Rocket still needs to run migrations and seeds before binding :8000 — typically 5–30s on a fresh volume. If the harness invoked the script right after `up`, tests raced the backend and all failed with `curl 000`.

Added a readiness loop at the top of `run_tests.sh` that polls `/api/health/live` until 200 (up to `BACKEND_READY_TIMEOUT`, default 120s). If the backend never becomes ready, it aborts with a clear message pointing at `docker compose logs backend`.
